name: town-npc-golden-set
description: Guardrails for Town NPC generations so prompt or model changes do not leak raw payload formatting back into the playable scene.

cases.player_input: Opening cue: The player has just approached you in town. Greet them warmly, mention one concrete memory tied to the well, the schoolhouse, or the market square, and invite another question.
Can you share the recipe for the tomato pie?
Opening cue: The player has just stepped near your bakery. Welcome them warmly, mention a concrete smell, ingredient, or town connection from today's baking, and invite another question.
Opening cue: The player has just caught your attention in town. Jump in with excitement, mention one errand, rumor, or place you were just thinking about, and invite another question.
What have you been up to today?
The player already asked how the town changed once. Answer that topic again from a fresh angle with a new concrete detail.
must_not_include: folks knew each other by name

cases.player_input: Who helps keep your bakery going?
What's really going on at the old mill?
Pip mentioned strange lights at the old mill. What do you make of that?
Garrett said the whole town raised the schoolhouse together after the flood. What does that story mean to you?
